feed.title: SAP Community - Rekha D R
feed.subtitle: Rekha D R in SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Service Item Cost Element for External Operation with PM03 control key
entry.content:
Hello Maria,

Thank you for your answer. in the ML90, the field is set as optional.

So configuration looks good. In the EAM_USER txn I set the default cost element .

It is working now. But it is not the solution if I want a different cost element.

Best Regards,

Rekha

entry.title: Re: Service Item Cost Element for External Operation with PM03 control key
entry.content:
Hello Maria,

I had set the Material Group, Purchasing group. Still error comes up. I am working with SAP PM Fiori based WebDynpro apps.

Best Regards,

Rekha

entry.title: Re: Service Item Cost Element for External Operation with PM03 control key
entry.content: Functionality wise, I wanted to understand, it is required to maintain cost element at service item level ? I case of order, cost element is picked up. But in case of Task List error comes up.

entry.title: Re: Service Item Cost Element for External Operation with PM03 control key
entry.content:
Hello Maria,

Thank you for your inputs. Maintaining cost element in EAM_USER txn also works. It is taken as default.

I came across this blog.

https://blogs.sap.com/2013/06/06/cost-element-determination-logic-for-pm03-operation/

I came across this link . By maintaining accounts for the

'Offsetting entry for inventory posting – GBB' in the txn OBYC (Configuration Accounting display), the issue got solved. Now the service item does not issue the error.

In Function Module MR_ACCOUNT_ASSIGNMENT, from table T030, account(cost element) is determined based on the Chart of accounts and other parameters.

Best Regards,

Rekha

entry.title: Invalid key predicate for Edm:DateTime
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am getting an error, Invalid key predicate for the Edm.DateTime field.

It has been annotated to be nullable. Still getting this error. Any inputs will be appreciated .

Please refer to screenshots for more details .

2018-07-31-10-46-21.jpg

Best Regards,

Rekha

entry.title: How to take over both Key and Description into input Field
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have a F4 help annotated in the CDS view for Fiori Elements List Report. In the Quick Actions Dialog, I am using a Field with F4 help, I have a Requirement that both key and description should be taken over into the Input field once the value is selected from the F4 help Result list. How can I achieve this ?

Best Regards,

Rekha f4-help-key-description.jpg

entry.title: Re: How to take over both Key and Description into input Field
entry.content:
Hello Tejas,

Thank you for the inputs. The semantics and Foreign key association has been used in the CDS view.

However, if you can take a look at the attachment, in the UI I need to take over description along

with the key from the F4 help results into input field. Right now only key is taken over.

Best Regards,

Rekha

entry.title: Re: How to take over both Key and Description into input Field
entry.content:
Hello Tejas,

Thank you for the suggestion. I have been using the std interface view I_PMNotificationPriority ( via association _PMNotificationPriority). Semantic.text : true has been added on MaintPriorityDesc field and used as text element for MaintPriority as shown below. Even after these, only key is taken over( As shown in the origianl post). Both CDS view and Data binding are as shown. I am using the change event( right now empty) . can anything be done in the change event so that even description can be taken over along with key?

entry.title: Re: How to take over both Key and Description into input Field
entry.content:
Hello Tejas,

Thank you for the inputs.

@UI.hidden annotation is on field level. It is only for ColorCode attribute and does not apply to MaintPriorityDescription.

Still I remove it and tried. No Luck so far. Same behaviour as before.

If there is any way to do bring ID/description together( In Controller) once the value is selected from the F4 help result list, it would be good.

Best Regards,

Rekha

entry.title: Re: The most important SAP skills for 2023 - and how to gain them
entry.content: Thank you so much lars.satow for all the efforts you have put into consolidating this highly valuable information. I am sure it will help many of us to Focus and upskill on what is important for the Future to thrive in SAP World..Keep enlightening us...!

entry.title: 🟣 Custom UI & Theming Essentials in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: In this session, we will explore the foundation of theming capabilities in SAP Build Apps. Covering the basics, we'll discuss smart colors, theme variables, and component style classes. To bring these concepts to life, we'll use an example application as a hands-on guide to modify its UI.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2023 Contest
entry.content: Hi @Nidhi : Can you try https://people.sap.com/ . What is your User Name here ? Can you take screenshot and paste here ?

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest Day 2023!!!
entry.content:
Create Better Tomorrow for Pets (BTP)..Create SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) for our Customers!

Create Better Tomorrow for our Pets (BTP)..Create SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) for our Customers!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Gaming Night Discussion
entry.content:
As a Child I used to play Mario on Computer and Snake Game on my father's Nokia Phone.

Played Poker for the First time. It was so much fun playing with other participants ! Thank you @eshrinivasan for hosting this fun game!

entry.title: 🇵🇭 SAP CodeJam on SAP Build: Create Apps, Processes & Sites Without Coding (Manila, Philippines)
entry.content:
Note: We have reached our maximum registration capacity and RSVP for this event is now closed. For those who could not sign up this time, please wait for the next announcement in this region.

This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of SAP Build, specifically about creating apps, business processes and business sites without coding.

You will get to know the environment by creating:

  • A sales order approval process (SAP Build Process Automation).
  • An app that lets users specify sales order data and trigger that process (SAP Build Apps).
  • A business site in which you will embed that app (SAP Build Work Zone).

You will get to know the SAP Build lobby, where you access all your SAP Build Artifacts, and get a deep understanding of all 3 SAP Build tools and how they work together.

  • SAP Build Process Automation: Learn how to drag and drop activities to build business processes in minutes, how to use the Store to reuse pre-built content, how to trigger the processes, and how to send approval forms to users.
  • SAP Build Apps: Learn how to drag and drop components to build UIs in minutes, create logic with flow functions, enable SAP BTP authentication, use destinations to access SAP backend data and services, and more.
  • SAP Build Work Zone: Learn how to create a business site and workspace in minutes, how to add all types of widgets, how to embed a Web Dynpro ABAP applications, how to embed SAP Build apps, and how to create integration cards to display backend business data. 

Pre-requisites:

Please bring your own laptop with power adapter. A supported Web browser such as Google Chrome, Firefox etc. You will be provided login credentials during the CodeJam and the underlying BTP environment is already configured with required services. An outline of the CodeJam is available in our SAP Build Code Jam repo.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday, November 23rd, 2023 from 09:00 - 16:00 hours PHT. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Address: 2F Boardroom, Building J, UP-Ayala Land Technohub, Commonwealth Avenue, Diliman Quezon City, Philippines 1121

The instructors are Developer Advocates @eshrinivasan and @Rekha_DR .

The event is kindly being hosted by IBM, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited – we can only accommodate 25 attendees. If you are willing to attend, register by clicking Yes button in the Will you be attending? section.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Foodtoberfest!!!
entry.content: Best Variety of healthy and Colorful food at SAP Labs India!

entry.title: Re: Desktoberfest - this year as part of the Scavenger Hunt!
entry.content: My work from home Set-up! My child sometimes plays around me 🙂

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Foodtoberfest!!!
entry.content: Glad to hear you love Indian Food 🙂 Looking forward to Meet you at TechEd Bangalore @Vitaliy-R

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam on SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding
entry.content:
Note: We have reached our maximum registration capacity and RSVP for this event and the resgistration is now closed. For those who could not sign up this time, please wait for the next announcement in this region.

This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of SAP Build, specifically about creating apps and business processes without coding (Low Code/No Code). 

You will get to know the environment by creating:

  • A Business process (SAP Build Process Automation).
  • A Business App  (SAP Build Apps).
  • (Demo) A business site in which you can embed the app (SAP Build Work Zone).

    You will get to know the SAP Build lobby, where you access all your SAP Build Artifacts, and get a deep understanding of all 3 SAP Build tools and how they work together.

    • SAP Build Process Automation: Learn how to drag and drop activities to build business processes in minutes, how to trigger the processes, and how to send approval forms to users.
    • SAP Build Apps: Learn how to drag and drop components to build UIs in minutes, enable SAP BTP authentication, use destinations to access SAP backend data and services, and more.
    • SAP Build Work Zone: Learn about business site and workspace in a Demo. 

 

Pre-requisites:

Please bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome, Firefox etc. installed on your laptop.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday, February 16th, 2024 from 09:30 - 16:00 hours IST. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Event Address: Walmart Global Tech - Pardhanani Wilshire II (PW2), Cessna business park, Kaverappa Layout, Kadubeesanahalli, Kadabeesanahalli, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560103. Meeting Room: "DC-PW-11F-Training Room-01"

The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @eshrinivasan and @Rekha_DR .

The event is kindly being hosted by Walmart Global Tech, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited – we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you are willing to attend, Register by clicking Yes button in the Will you be attending? section.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Invite to take part in "SAP Build CodeJam" at Walmart Global Tech, Bengaluru, INDIA
entry.content:
Hello SAP Developers,
Are you interested in Learning about SAP BTP Build? SAP Developer Advocates are conducting "SAP Build CodeJam" at Walmart Global Tech, Bengaluru, India.
Please follow the link
https://groups.community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/sap-codejam-on-sap-build-create-apps-and-processes-without-coding/ev-p/310747 to Register and take part in the event and for more details...

Thank you and Best Regards,

Rekha DR
